what: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: lssdoc turns LimeSurvey .lss export polished Word (.docx) PDF questionnaire document anyone working LimeSurvey survey: researchers, survey methodologists, ethics committees, translators reviewers. renders questionnaire content side side four languages, runs automated integrity audit, produces layout reads published instrument – developer dump. Two output templates: "cards" – one detached block per item (meta table + question table), stacked vertically. Closest printed questionnaire respondent see.  "table" – one dense codebook table covering whole document: every variable one tinted Question row carrying metadata, followed one white Value rows holding answer codes labels per language. Group banners, welcome text, description end text become labelled rows table codebook reads single artifact.  things lssdoc takes care : Multilingual chrome. Column headers, row labels, type labels (Single choice / Multiple choice / Text / Number …), Value descriptors, audit section – everything outside survey content – translated English, French, German, Spanish Italian. Independent survey’s content languages, can keep FR/DE questionnaire switch document scaffolding English (vice versa) single argument. Variable-centric compound rendering. Array, multiple-choice, multiple-numerical dual-scale array questions documented per subquestion, real variable code (parent_subq) meta row – parent code never appears data export. Methodological type labels. UI distinctions like “List (radio)” vs “List (dropdown)” collapse “Single choice”, response semantics identical actual codes appear Value section. Type cell reads methodologist’s vocabulary, LimeSurvey’s. Automated audit. Missing translations, duplicate codes, forward references routing filters, array-scale inconsistencies, orphan structural references, types missing required options subquestions – flagged inline rendered item , ask , dedicated section. AI; rule-based reviewable. Local-processing. network calls, third-party services. questionnaire content never leaves machine R runs.

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: development version, GitHub, pak (recommended): remotes: package depends officer flextable (declared Suggests – parse audit paths work without ).

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: public API four functions: audit_lss(), render_questionnaire() render_audit() accept input either path .lss file pre-parsed lss object. output format two renderers inferred extension output (.docx .pdf).

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: output uses "cards" template default, chrome_lang following survey’s primary content language. Open review.docx Word, click Yes prompted update fields (page numbers table contents).
code: library(lssdoc)  # Parse the .lss and render the Word document in a single call. render_questionnaire("survey.lss", "review.docx")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: languages controls content columns appear order; first one primary language used table contents group headings. chrome_lang independent languages: keep chrome_lang = "en" get English column headers row labels even survey content runs French German – pass chrome_lang = "fr" ("de" / "es" / "") want scaffolding follow content.

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: cover page picks authors block (clickable ORCID hyperlinks), description (URLs auto-detected made clickable), privacy / admin settings rows metadata table.
code: render_questionnaire(   "survey.lss",   "ethics_review.docx",   languages              = c("en", "fr"),   template               = "table",   chrome_lang            = "en",   show_privacy_settings  = TRUE,   # anonymized / save partial / IP / referrer / timestamp   show_admin_settings    = TRUE,   # alias / end URL / active   authors = list(     list(name        = "Amal Tawfik",          affiliation = "HES-SO Valais-Wallis",          orcid       = "0009-0006-2422-1555")   ),   description = paste0(     "Validated as part of the SNSF project XYZ. ",     "Reference: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.123456"   ) )

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: Pure data: one row per variable header, one row per value code, nothing else.
code: render_questionnaire(   "survey.lss",   "minimal.docx",   template         = "table",   show_welcome     = FALSE,   show_endtext     = FALSE,   show_description = FALSE,   show_groups      = FALSE,   show_toc         = FALSE,   show_audit       = FALSE,   show_index       = FALSE )

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
code: # Parse a single time, then audit and render different variants # without re-reading the .lss. lss   <- read_lss("survey.lss") audit <- audit_lss(lss) print(audit)  # console summary of flagged anomalies  render_questionnaire(lss, "review_en.docx", languages = "en") render_questionnaire(lss, "review_fr.docx", languages = "fr") render_audit(lss, "qa_followup.docx")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: deliberately flawed survey ships package, can see audit_lss() catches without supplying file:
code: demo <- system.file("extdata", "audit_demo.lss", package = "lssdoc") audit_lss(read_lss(demo)) #> -- lssdoc audit ---------------------------------------------------- #> 11 findings: 4 errors, 7 warnings, 0 notes. #> x Question 'age': filter references 'income', asked later (forward reference) #> x Survey: duplicate question code 'age' #> x Subquestion 'orphan_sq': points to a question id that does not exist #> ! Question 'satisf': expects answer options, but none are defined #> ! Question 'income': text missing in 'fr' #> ! Question 'comment ': code carries whitespace #> ... plus empty texts, missing subquestions and an array-scale mismatch.

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
code: # Same call, just pass a .pdf path. The package writes the .docx # into a temp file and converts it locally via LibreOffice # (must be installed and on PATH). render_questionnaire("survey.lss", "review.pdf", template = "table") render_audit("survey.lss", "qa.pdf")

title: lssdoc: Word and PDF questionnaire documents from LimeSurvey '.lss' files
text: rendered .docx uses editorial petrol-blue palette tuned publication-grade output (#133B52 primary, #3A7C8C accent, #E9F2F6 #F4F8FA tinted bands, #D3DCE2 soft grid). Calibri body / Consolas monospace default; font font_code arguments accept string override.

what: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: Inspect LimeSurvey survey flag anomalies can detected without AI. audit guides human reviewer; silently correct anything. Every finding names precise location severity.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: input Either path .lss file (character string) pre-parsed lss object returned read_lss(). Passing path parses fly; passing lss object avoids re-parsing survey also rendered session.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: object class lss_audit: list file, languages, summary counts, findings data frame (severity, check, location, language, message). print() method .data.frame() method.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
text: Checks performed: Missing translations – question, help, answer, subquestion text present least one language empty another. Empty languages – translatable text empty every language. Duplicate codes – question variable code repeated survey, answer/subquestion code repeated within one question. Whitespace codes – question, subquestion answer code containing leading, trailing interior whitespace (likely typo; causes subtle bugs data export). Missing options type – question whose type requires answer options subquestions none (per type taxonomy). Forward filter references – relevance expression names variable appearing filtered question (value yet collected filter evaluated). Array-scale inconsistencies – array (single dual) whose subquestions reference scale_id answer options, vice versa. Orphan references – subquestion answer pointing question exist.

title: Audit a LimeSurvey survey for reviewable anomalies — audit_lss
code: audit <- audit_lss(system.file("extdata", "hesav_2026.lss",                                package = "lssdoc")) print(audit) #>  #> ── lssdoc audit ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> File: /tmp/RtmpcPgRKk/temp_libpath1a1d3b4e6764/lssdoc/extdata/hesav_2026.lss #> Languages: "de" and "fr" #> ✔ No anomalies detected.

what: lssdoc: Render Multilingual Questionnaires from LimeSurvey '.lss' Files — lssdoc-package
title: lssdoc: Render Multilingual Questionnaires from LimeSurvey '.lss' Files — lssdoc-package
text: Render LimeSurvey '.lss' survey exports multilingual questionnaire documents Word ('.docx') PDF, displaying four languages side side localized chrome English, French, German, Spanish Italian. Includes rule-based automated audit flags missing translations, forward filter references, duplicate codes, array-scale inconsistencies orphan structural references. Designed researchers, methodologists, ethics committees translators reviewing multilingual surveys. Processing fully local: source file input questionnaire content uploaded third-party service.

title: lssdoc: Render Multilingual Questionnaires from LimeSurvey '.lss' Files — lssdoc-package

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
text: Pretty-printed audit summary console, capped first n findings. Severity-based bullet symbols (errors, warnings, notes) mirror shown audit table inside rendered .docx.

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
code: # S3 method for class 'lss_audit' print(x, ..., n = 20L)

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
text: x lss_audit object returned audit_lss(). ... Currently ignored. n Maximum number findings print. Defaults 20. Set Inf print every finding. remaining count, , summarized bottom hint use .data.frame() full list.

title: Print an lss_audit object — print.lss_audit
text: audit object, invisibly.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: Read LimeSurvey survey structure export (.lss, XML file) turn structured lss object rest package can audit (audit_lss()) render (render_questionnaire(), render_audit()). Parsing fully local: file never uploaded anywhere.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: file Character. Path .lss file. Must single string pointing existing file, otherwise classed error raised (lssdoc_bad_path, lssdoc_file_not_found).

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: object class lss: list survey languages, metadata, one data frame per .lss section. Structural sections (surveys, groups, questions, subquestions, answers, question_attributes, conditions) stay separate localized text sections (survey_language_settings, group_l10ns, question_l10ns, answer_l10ns), carry per-language titles, labels, help texts. values read verbatim character.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss
text: .lss format LimeSurvey XML export. Since DBVersion 4xx/7xx translatable text lives dedicated localization sections (*_l10ns), keyed language, structural sections hold identifiers settings. read_lss() reads every section tidy data frame without mutating user-facing identifier text. field present empty (e.g. <help/>) read ""; field absent row read NA.

title: Read a LimeSurvey .lss file — read_lss

what: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
text: Build short, action-oriented document containing audit findings: cover page full questionnaire document, summary counts, one table per severity (errors, warnings, notes) listing every finding location message. Use QA follow-share issues colleague without distributing full questionnaire.

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
code: render_audit(   input,   output,   languages = NULL,   logo = NULL,   logo_width = 1.5,   logo_height = 0.75,   font = NULL,   font_code = NULL,   colors = NULL,   authors = NULL,   description = NULL,   chrome_lang = NULL )

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
text: input Either path .lss file (character string) pre-parsed lss object returned read_lss(). Passing path parses fly; passing lss object avoids re-parsing workflow already inspected audit. output Character. Path file create. extension determines output format: .docx writes Word document directly, .pdf writes Word document temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice (Word, Windows). extension rejected lssdoc_bad_output_ext. languages Character vector language codes used cover page. NULL (default) keeps languages survey declared order. logo Optional path (character) PNG JPEG image displayed top cover page. NULL (default) keeps cover logo-free. logo_width, logo_height Image dimensions inches. Defaults 1.5 0.75, tuned 2:1 logo. Resize pre-crop image fit different aspect ratio. font Optional body font name (character). NULL (default) keeps Calibri. See render_questionnaire() guidance overrides. font_code Optional monospace font (character) used code-like content (variable codes, raw expressions). NULL (default) keeps Consolas. colors Optional named list hex color overrides editorial petrol-blue palette. NULL (default) keeps package palette intact. shape accepted names render_questionnaire(). authors, description Optional cover-page credit block (authors) free-form note (description). NULL (default) . shapes render_questionnaire(). chrome_lang Language used document chrome (column headers, row labels, audit section). One "en", "fr", "de", "es", "". NULL (default) follows languages[1] supported, otherwise falls back "en".

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
text: output path, invisibly.

title: Render the audit as a focused Word or PDF document — render_audit
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # One-shot (path -> .docx) render_audit(   system.file("extdata", "limesurvey_survey_751689.lss",               package = "lssdoc"),   tempfile(fileext = ".docx") )  # PDF output -- same call, just pass a .pdf path render_audit("survey.lss", "qa.pdf") } # }

what: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: Build professional questionnaire document LimeSurvey survey, displaying four languages side side. question becomes compact flextable meta header (variable code, type, mandatory, filter) shown , language column headers, question text per language, subquestion answer-option rows underneath – codes left, labels per language right. Headings, metadata cover page, optional table contents, optional audit summary tie document together. Rendering uses suggested packages officer flextable; must installed.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
code: render_questionnaire(   input,   output,   languages = NULL,   template = c("cards", "table"),   layout = c("auto", "side-by-side", "stacked"),   show_audit = TRUE,   show_help = TRUE,   show_attrs = c("prefix", "suffix", "other_replace_text", "validation"),   show_technical_attrs = FALSE,   page_format = c("auto", "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape", "A3"),   show_toc = TRUE,   show_index = TRUE,   show_header_title = TRUE,   show_source = TRUE,   show_item_heading = FALSE,   show_raw_filter = FALSE,   show_groups = TRUE,   show_welcome = TRUE,   show_endtext = TRUE,   show_description = TRUE,   show_privacy_settings = FALSE,   show_admin_settings = FALSE,   title = NULL,   logo = NULL,   logo_width = 1.5,   logo_height = 0.75,   font = NULL,   font_code = NULL,   colors = NULL,   authors = NULL,   description = NULL,   chrome_lang = NULL )

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: input Either path .lss file (character string) pre-parsed lss object returned read_lss(). Passing path parses fly. Passing lss object avoids re-parsing survey rendered different options (e.g. multiple language subsets) session. output Character. Path file create. extension determines output format: .docx writes Word document directly, .pdf writes Word document temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice (Word, Windows). extension rejected lssdoc_bad_output_ext. languages Character vector language codes display, order appear columns. NULL (default) keeps languages found .lss file order <languages> section. Acts subset filter ordering: e.g. c("en", "fr") puts English first, c("fr", "en") puts French first. languages[1] treated primary language (TOC entries, group fallback). Requesting language absent survey error (lssdoc_unknown_language). template Output style. One "cards" (default) "table". "cards" renders one detached pair tables per item (meta table + item table), stacked vertically, content languages displayed side--side item table. "table" renders single dense table covering whole document: every variable one tinted Question row carrying | Variable | Type | Mand. | Filter, followed one white Value rows. Group banners become merged section rows; column header repeats every page. Auto-promotes page format A4 landscape 2+ languages (override via page_format). layout Reserved future use. Currently "auto" . show_audit Logical. TRUE (default), include audit summary section near top inline markers questions carry findings. Set FALSE clean reading copy. show_help Logical. TRUE (default), include question help texts question text. show_attrs Character vector question attributes surface question text present. Default keeps attributes change respondents see item: "prefix", "suffix", "other_replace_text", "validation". Add "exclude_all_others" "exclude_all_others_auto" also surface row-level exclusivity flags (debug-style). Pass character(0) hide . show_technical_attrs Logical. TRUE, include technical attributes answer_order location_*. FALSE (default) hides . page_format Page format. One "auto" (default), "A4-portrait", "A4-landscape", "A3". "auto" picks portrait one two languages landscape three. table template auto-promotes A4 landscape two languages – pass explicit value override. show_toc Logical. TRUE (default), include table contents listing groups (skipped automatically survey fewer two groups). per-variable navigation, use show_index. show_index Logical. TRUE (default), append variable index end document listing every item code number, sorted alphabetically. show_header_title Logical. TRUE (default), show survey title top right every page (one line per displayed language, truncated 80 characters). FALSE keeps X/Y page counter bottom right. show_source Logical. TRUE (default), show Source file name Survey ID rows cover metadata table. Pass FALSE hide (e.g. sharing without exposing LimeSurvey internals). show_item_heading Logical. FALSE (default), meta table starts item directly, compact layout. TRUE, bold "N. variable" heading added item scroll-time navigation. show_raw_filter Logical. FALSE (default), Filter cell shows human-readable form (e.g. Q1 = 1) – editorial codebook style, matching ESS / MOSAiCH / GESIS conventions. Set TRUE also surface raw LimeSurvey relevance expression underneath small italic gray (e.g. !is_empty(Q1.NAOK) && (Q1.NAOK == 1)), useful QA cross-checks. raw form always shown plain form simplified. show_groups Logical. TRUE (default), show group banners (cards layout) group rows (table layout). Pass FALSE flatten document single sequence items section breaks (useful groups exist internal organization). show_welcome Logical. TRUE (default), include survey's multilingual welcome text (surveyls_welcometext) side--side block (cards) embedded row (table). show_endtext Logical. TRUE (default), include survey's multilingual end text (surveyls_endtext). treatment show_welcome. show_description Logical. TRUE (default), include survey's multilingual description (surveyls_description) – "survey " intro LimeSurvey shows welcome text landing page. show_privacy_settings Logical. FALSE (default), omit survey-level privacy / tracking flags cover. Set TRUE surface anonymized, save partial, datestamp, ipaddr, refurl rows – useful ethics committee submissions. show_admin_settings Logical. FALSE (default), omit survey-level administrative settings. Set TRUE surface alias, end URL description, active flag rows. title Optional override survey title shown cover top-right header. NULL (default) uses per-language titles .lss survey settings. Pass single string use title every displayed language, named character vector keyed language code (e.g. c(fr = "Mon titre", de = "Mein Titel")) per-language overrides. logo Optional path (character) PNG JPEG image displayed top cover page. NULL (default) keeps cover logo-free, matching neutral style survey-methodology references (ESS, MOSAiCH, Panel). .lss file embed logo, image must supplied caller. logo_width, logo_height Image dimensions inches. Defaults 1.5 0.75, tuned 2:1 logo. Resize pre-crop image fit different aspect ratio. font Optional body font name (character). NULL (default) keeps Calibri, pre-installed every recent Windows Office metric-substituted Carlito (OFL) Mac Linux LibreOffice, column widths stay stable across platforms. Pass string override (e.g. "Source Sans 3", "IBM Plex Sans", corporate brand font); install font machine opens document, otherwise reader's application substitutes fallback face. font_code Optional monospace font name (character) used code-like content: variable column meta table, raw relevance expression filter cell, variable index entries. NULL (default) keeps Consolas; pass "JetBrains Mono" "IBM Plex Mono" sharper code style. colors Optional named list hex color overrides editorial petrol-blue palette. NULL (default) keeps package palette intact. Accepted names: "primary" (group filets, headers text item top borders), "accent" (hyperlinks, ORCID iD, URL auto-links, group -line cards), "band" (light header backgrounds), "band_dark" (meta-table dark header cards), "zebra" (-light tint Question rows table template), "grid" (0.5 pt border color), "text", "muted". value must hex string ("#XXXXXX" "#XXX"). Unknown keys rejected (lssdoc_bad_colors). Useful honoring institutional brand: e.g. colors = list(primary = "#5C9F1A", accent = "#7FA82E") produces LimeSurvey-green document. authors Optional credit block questionnaire's designers, displayed cover page subtitle. author shown centered line Name -- Affiliation; ORCID iD provided, smaller monospace line shows ORCID 0000-0000-0000-0000 hyperlink https://orcid.org/<id>. Accepts: NULL (default): authorship block. unnamed character vector (c("Amal Tawfik", "John Doe")): entry becomes line affiliation. named character vector (c("Amal Tawfik" = "HES-Valais")): names authors, values affiliations. Use "" render author without affiliation. list named lists full form, e.g. list(list(name = "Amal Tawfik", affiliation = "HES-Valais", orcid = "0009-0006-2422-1555"), list(name = "John Doe", affiliation = "UNIL")). name field required; affiliation orcid optional. description Optional free-form text (single string) shown cover page authors block. NULL (default) omits block. Useful citation hint, funding acknowledgement, methodology note, link related publication. Line breaks (\n) split block separate centered lines; http:// https:// tokens rendered clickable hyperlinks. chrome_lang Language used chrome document (column headers, row labels, navigation titles, type labels, Value descriptors, audit section). One "en", "fr", "de", "es", "". NULL (default) follows languages[1] supported, otherwise falls back "en". Independent languages, controls survey's content columns: e.g. chrome_lang = "en" languages = c("fr", "en") produces English-labelled document French English content. Spanish Italian translations reviewed native speaker publishing official document.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: output path, invisibly.

what: "LimeSurvey last save" date on the cover
title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: cover metadata table carries row labelled "LimeSurvey last save" (localized equivalent). read verbatim surveys.lastmodified column .lss, timestamp LimeSurvey writes export – table (questions, question_l10ns, answer_l10ns, groups, etc.) carries per-row modification date. row named "last save" rather "last modified" LimeSurvey bumps field reliably user clicks Save survey-level form (Settings tab); editing question text, answer label, translation Question Editor consistently update across LimeSurvey versions. date looks stale relative recent edits, workaround open Survey settings LimeSurvey, click Save (change needed), re-export .lss. next render show bumped timestamp.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: Opening rendered .docx Microsoft Word may surface security-style prompt: "document contains fields may refer files. want update fields document?". expected: package marks page-number bookmark-reference fields needing refresh footer shows correct page count table contents links resolve right pages first open (also makes headless PDF conversion via LibreOffice produce correctly paginated output without manual F9). Clicking Yes safe – document INCLUDETEXT, INCLUDEPICTURE-linked, DDE fields; external links ORCID DOI URLs cover credits, static HYPERLINK targets fetched update.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
text: output ends .pdf, function first renders .docx temporary location converts locally via LibreOffice headless (Word Windows). LibreOffice (soffice executable) must installed PATH; otherwise classed error explains install . Conversion stays user's machine: upload, network call. LibreOffice headless refresh Word field values (TOC, page counts) conversion, table contents may appear empty converted PDF. obtain PDF populated TOC, render .docx instead, open Word (TOC refreshes automatically) use File > Save > PDF.

title: Render a LimeSurvey questionnaire to a Word or PDF document — render_questionnaire
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ file <- system.file("extdata", "hesav_2026.lss", package = "lssdoc")  # One-shot: parse + render Word document render_questionnaire(file, tempfile(fileext = ".docx"))  # Same call, PDF output (format inferred from extension) render_questionnaire(file, tempfile(fileext = ".pdf"))  # Parse once, render several variants without re-parsing lss <- read_lss(file) render_questionnaire(lss, tempfile(fileext = ".docx"),                      languages = "en") render_questionnaire(lss, tempfile(fileext = ".docx"),                      template = "table",                      languages = c("en", "fr"))  # Branded cover with authors block and palette override render_questionnaire(   lss,   tempfile(fileext = ".docx"),   template    = "table",   chrome_lang = "en",   colors      = list(primary = "#5C9F1A", accent = "#7FA82E"),   authors     = list(list(     name        = "Amal Tawfik",     affiliation = "HES-SO Valais-Wallis",     orcid       = "0009-0006-2422-1555"   )) ) } # }

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: First release. lssdoc reads LimeSurvey .lss export renders multilingual questionnaire document (Word .docx PDF) review ethics committees, methodologists, researchers translators, together rule-based audit survey. user-facing API four functions: read_lss(), audit_lss(), render_questionnaire() render_audit(). processing stays user’s machine. Tested across two example surveys, templates (cards table), five chrome languages (English, French, German, Spanish, Italian) output formats.

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: read_lss() reads LimeSurvey .lss file structured lss object, preserving user text verbatim. validates .lss DBVersion tested window (400 799): version 400 (pre *_l10ns schema) raises classed error, version 800 warns still parses. audit_lss(input) two renderers accept either path .lss file pre-parsed lss object input.

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: audit_lss() inspects parsed survey reports reviewable anomalies classed lss_audit object print method: missing translations, texts empty every language, duplicate question answer codes, types missing required options subquestions, orphan references. also flags forward filter references – routing condition depends variable appearing filtered question, almost always survey-design bug (severity: error); array-scale inconsistencies – array whose subquestions answer options use mismatched scale_id values (severity: warning); whitespace identifier codes – question, subquestion answer code carrying leading, trailing interior whitespace, causes silent lookup failures data export (severity: warning). print(audit) paginates output 20 findings default, hint use print(audit, n = Inf) .data.frame(audit) see .

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: render_questionnaire() produces Word PDF questionnaire document path parsed lss: cover page survey title every language metadata table, table contents, optional audit section near top inline markers affected questions, group sections, one compact block per question meta header (number, variable code, type, mandatory, filter) one column per language question text, subquestions answer options. output format (.docx .pdf) inferred output extension; PDF produced locally via LibreOffice. page orientation auto-picks portrait (1-2 languages) landscape (3+). render_audit() produces focused audit-document: cover page, one section per severity (errors, warnings, notes) table findings. Use QA follow-, separate full review. "cards" (default) – meta band plus item table per question, stacked vertically, reading questionnaire. "table" – one dense codebook table covering whole document, one tinted Question row per variable one Value rows beneath. chrome_lang controls document chrome (column headers, row labels, audit-section labels) independently survey content languages. Supported: "en", "fr", "de", "es", "".

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: cover shows LimeSurvey Survey ID last-save timestamp stable traceability. Optional logo places PNG JPEG image top cover page (default keeps cover logo-free, matching neutral style survey-methodology references). Optional authors supports unnamed character vector, named character vector (name = affiliation), list named lists (name, affiliation, orcid). ORCID iDs render monospace hyperlinks. Optional description shown authors block. Line breaks (\n) split separate centered lines; http:// https:// tokens render clickable hyperlinks. Multilingual welcome text, endtext description toggleable via show_welcome, show_endtext, show_description (TRUE default). Optional show_privacy_settings show_admin_settings surface LimeSurvey flags (anonymized, save partial, datestamp, ipaddr, refurl, alias, end URL, active) additional cover metadata rows (default FALSE). show_toc, show_index show_header_title (TRUE) add group table contents (skipped fewer two groups), alphabetical variable index listing every item code number, survey title top every page. page footer always carries compact X/Y page counter.

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: Filter cell reads chosen chrome language – , , answered, empty matches localize alongside rest chrome. humanizer translates LimeSurvey’s defensive !is_empty(X.NAOK) && (X.NAOK OP value) idiom X OP value. collapses several disjunctions one variable set notation (Q1 = 1 Q1 = 2 Q1 = 3 reads Q1 {1, 2, 3}) two bounds encased range (age >= 18 age <= 65 reads 18 <= age <= 65, Unicode math symbols output). also unwraps intval(X.NAOK), maps strlen(X.NAOK) predicates “answered” / “empty”, renders regexMatch("pat", X.NAOK) X matches "pat", drops .X group prefixes. show_raw_filter defaults FALSE: Filter cell shows human-readable form (e.g. Q1 = 1), matching ESS / MOSAiCH / GESIS conventions. Pass show_raw_filter = TRUE also surface raw LimeSurvey expression underneath.

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: cards body renders one uniform type size two-tone meta band cell-symmetric alignment per column (column’s header takes alignment body). meta table, intra-item gap item table carry Word’s “keep next” property stay page typical case. table template language columns get least 50% page width, Mandatory header abbreviates dense layout (Mand. / Oblig. / Pflicht / Obblig.), body font steps (8 pt 7 pt) rendering 3 4 languages narrower columns stay readable. package ships hex logo (man/figures/logo.png .svg) favicon set pkgdown site.

title: lssdoc 0.1.0
text: Continuous integration runs full render suite; Codecov coverage badge README wired .github/workflows/test-coverage.yaml.
